title: Lucid Group (LCID) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Peter Rawlinson stepped down as CEO and transitioned to strategic technical advisor to the board chair. Marc Winterhoff became interim CEO. Taoufiq Boussaid joined as new CFO. Gagan Dhingra was promoted to Senior VP of Finance. In 2024, production reached 9,029 vehicles, up 7% year-over-year. Deliveries were 10,241 vehicles, up 71% year-over-year, a record year. Gross margin improved from negative 225% in 2023 to negative 114% in 2024. Lucid Air was the number one best-selling EV in its segment during the second half of 2024 and third among all vehicles in its class. For 2025, production guidance was approximately 20,000 vehicles. Gross margin was expected to show significant improvement similar to 2024 trajectory. Capital expenditures were expected to be approximately $1.4 billion. Liquidity was $6.13 billion, providing runway into second half of 2026. Lucid Gravity orders exceeded Air Grand Touring orders in the same timeframe. Over 75% of Gravity orders came from new customers. Majority were configured above $120,000. Hands-free driving was expected to roll out later in 2025. Midsize platform production start was scheduled for late 2026. Advanced engineering phase was completed for two midsize variants. Tariff and regulatory credit impacts were estimated at 7-12% potential gross margin impact. Saudi Arabia partnership included up to 100,000 vehicles with option for another 50,000, primarily midsize and Gravity focus.
